chief_complaint: A 45-year-old female patient presented with visual impairment and an abnormal hormone profile.
history: The patient underwent an endoscopic endonasal approach (EEA) for infundibulo-neurohypophysitis. Postoperative, she developed a bi-thalamic stroke due to a Percheron artery infarct.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80 mmHg, HR 80 bpm, RR 16 breaths/min. Physical examination revealed visual impairment and signs of endocrine dysfunction.
labs: ECG: normal. Bloods: elevated inflammatory markers, abnormal hormone profile. Imaging: MRI showing bi-thalamic stroke and Percheron artery variant.
correct_diagnosis: Percheron artery infarct secondary to endoscopic endonasal approach for infundibulo-neurohypophysitis
differentials: Percheron artery infarct secondary to endoscopic endonasal approach for infundibulo-neurohypophysitis
correct_ranking: Percheron artery infarct secondary to endoscopic endonasal approach for infundibulo-neurohypophysitis
